meta.description: Education transformation proposals, scientific perspectives, dilemmas, and research questions.

topic.perspective_explainer: The proposal is the object. Disciplines are reusable evaluative perspectives—not simulated people.
topic.research_change_choice: Research that could change the choice
topic.migration_note: Deterministic corpus migration—not a fresh research run. Every proposal retains its legacy scenario id and audit path.
topic.evidence_value_note: Evidence can clarify consequences; it cannot rank these values.
topic.no_dilemma: No value dilemma was extracted for this proposal.

index.repeat_build: 2 decision packages · 12/12 reused steps on repeat build
index.hero_title: From debate to a library of change.
index.hero_deck: Evidence does not vote. It supports findings, tests transformations through scientific perspectives, and marks the choices that remain human.
index.live_title: Add one perspective. Watch only its descendants move.
index.live_deck: A full fresh round plus the PR #29 educational-psychology perspective: identical transformations, six new assessments, explicit position-carriage test.
index.migration_copy: This visible slice recompiles committed v1 research into the v2 graph. It proves the architecture and information model; it does not claim fresh research.

comparison.hero_deck: Same committed evidence corpus. Different unit of thought.
comparison.verdict_title: Rewrite the semantic core; preserve the evidence trail.
comparison.verdict_copy: The vertical slice validates the new graph, schemas, step cache, and public reading model. It does not yet validate new live-agent prompts or evidence quality beyond v1.
comparison.limit_one: No paid model call was made, so v2 generation quality is not being compared yet.
comparison.limit_two: Perspective assessments are deterministic projections of v1 expert records, not fresh disciplinary reviews.
comparison.limit_three: Source strings were preserved but not re-verified during migration.

production.title: Two questions, rebuilt from evidence
production.deck: Fresh research, explicit transformation coverage, scientific perspective assessments, dilemmas, and visible claim-to-source citations.
production.ready_with_conditions: ready for human review with conditions
production.not_comparable: Scores are not directly comparable; the rubric and architecture changed.

experiment.hero_title: What changes when psychology becomes a perspective?
experiment.hero_deck: Same evidence-derived transformations. One added disciplinary perspective. Only its descendants may change.
experiment.boundary_copy: Fresh research and generation; the psychology evidence is a PR #29 sensitivity test, not admitted knowledge.
experiment.option_title: The option space did not move. The interpretation did.
experiment.perspective_rule: The perspective evaluates proposals; it does not impersonate an expert or vote for a winner.
experiment.warning_title: The new information is a warning about access-only reform.
experiment.warning_copy: Quota and outreach can widen entry while leaving the psychological cost of the selective reference group intact. Delayed selection and de-tracking address that mechanism more directly.
experiment.warning_quote: Equally able students placed in a highly selective peer group can develop lower academic self-concept—even as measured achievement rises.
experiment.judge_copy: The −0.333 difference is descriptive, not a causal estimate. Psychology improved mechanism visibility, while evidence discipline and dilemma clarity each fell by one judge point in this sample.
experiment.audit_title: The failed carriage taught the contract
experiment.audit_copy: The first treatment package stopped at 316 words and dropped every named psychology mechanism. It remains in immutable lineage. A system-level 500–900 word and named-mechanism validator was added; the accepted rerun contains 664 words and passes carriage.
experiment.interpretation_copy: One stochastic pair cannot identify a stable score effect. It can validate dependency localization, record coverage, and whether a named disciplinary mechanism survives to the decision package.

t1.title: Status quo with monitoring
t1.impact: Monitoring does not change the psychological costs of early ranking, labeling, and reference groups.

t2.title: Shift selection to ages 14–16
t2.impact: Later selection means fewer early high-stakes rankings; the expected benefit remains implementation-dependent.

t3.title: Retain tracks, reform admissions
t3.impact: Admissions reform redistributes who bears the costs of selection more than it removes those costs.

t4.title: Catchment and school-choice desegregation
t4.impact: Catchment and choice reform indirectly changes composition, but the psychological mechanism of selection remains.

t5.title: Targeted quota and early outreach
t5.impact: A quota may widen access but also carries big-fish–little-pond risk: an elite reference group can lower academic self-concept.

t6.title: Comprehensive lower-secondary pilot
t6.impact: A de-tracking pilot addresses labeling and performance orientation most directly, but it is not a proven cure-all.
